content:
Generate a concise commit message for this diff. The commit wraps pace BOAAK. Output only the message, no quotes.
diff --git a/Tools/rbk/rbh0/rbhopw_payor_wrapper.sh b/Tools/rbk/rbh0/rbhopw_payor_wrapper.sh
index 50d4546b..b343ace0 100644
--- a/Tools/rbk/rbh0/rbhopw_payor_wrapper.sh
+++ b/Tools/rbk/rbh0/rbhopw_payor_wrapper.sh
@@ -91,6 +91,40 @@ rbho_payor_handbook() {
   buh_line "${RBYC_PAYOR} can list Depots for verification:"
   buh_tt  "  " "${RBZ_LIST_DEPOT}"
   buh_e
+
+  buh_line "At the end of a successful ${RBYC_LEVY}, the depot-immutable"
+  buh_line "settings in ${RBYC_RBRD} (cloud prefix, depot moniker, region, and"
+  buh_line "Cloud Build machine type) are inscribed into the Depot as a"
+  buh_line "tripwire image. Every later cloud build pulls that image and"
+  buh_line "byte-compares it against your local ${RBYC_RBRD} file before"
+  buh_line "submitting, so an accidental post-levy edit fails loud instead of"
+  buh_line "silently pointing builds at the wrong project or worker pool."
+  buh_e
+  buh_line "Three failure modes you may meet, and how to recover:"
+  buh_e
+  buh_warn "Drift — a cloud build dies reporting a ${RBYC_RBRD} mismatch."
+  buh_line "  Your local ${RBYC_RBRD} no longer matches what the Depot was"
+  buh_line "  levied with. Restore the file to its inscribed contents (the"
+  buh_line "  failure prints a diff), or if the change is intentional, unmake"
+  buh_line "  and re-levy the Depot — ${RBYC_RBRD} is frozen for its lifetime:"
+  buh_tt  "    " "${RBZ_UNMAKE_DEPOT}" "" " <depot-project-id>"
+  buh_tt  "    " "${RBZ_LEVY_DEPOT}"
+  buh_e
+  buh_warn "Missing tripwire — a cloud build dies reporting the image is absent."
+  buh_line "  The Depot was levied before the tripwire existed, or the image"
+  buh_line "  was removed. Inscribe it once against the current ${RBYC_RBRD}"
+  buh_line "  (supply a fresh access token):"
+  buh_tt  "    " "${RBZ_INSCRIBE_DEPOT}" "" " \$(gcloud auth print-access-token)"
+  buh_e
+  buh_warn "Re-inscribe refused — re-running levy on an inscribed Depot dies."
+  buh_line "  The tripwire is already present and is never overwritten in"
+  buh_line "  place. To refresh it, unmake the Depot and levy fresh:"
+  buh_tt  "    " "${RBZ_UNMAKE_DEPOT}" "" " <depot-project-id>"
+  buh_tt  "    " "${RBZ_LEVY_DEPOT}"
+  buh_e
+  buh_line "To verify alignment at any time without submitting a build:"
+  buh_tt  "  " "${RBZ_CHECK_DEPOT}" "" " \$(gcloud auth print-access-token)"
+  buh_e
   buh_line "${RBYC_PAYOR} creates the Governor service account:"
   buh_tt  "  " "${RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR}"
   buh_e
diff --git a/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc b/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc
index 903ce7a0..dece7b50 100644
--- a/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc
+++ b/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc
@@ -346,6 +346,7 @@
 :rbrd_group_depot_identity:  <<rbrd_group_depot_identity,Depot Identity Group>>
 :rbrd_group_region:          <<rbrd_group_region,GCP Region Group>>
 :rbrd_group_pool:            <<rbrd_group_pool,Cloud Build Pool Group>>
+:rbrd_tripwire:              <<rbrd_tripwire,RBRD Tripwire>>
 // Recipe Bottle GCP Constants (rbgc_*) — pure-literal tinder, available source-time
 :rbgc_depot_project_infix:   <<rbgc_depot_project_infix,RBGC_depot_project_infix>>
 
diff --git a/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc b/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc
index efcbd731..42ecb33f 100644
--- a/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc
+++ b/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc
@@ -245,6 +245,16 @@ follow-up hint
 {rbtoe_depot_list_update}
 to refresh per-moniker depot fact files
 
+//axhoot_typed_output
+{rbtgi_depot}
+. *Inscribe Depot Regime Tripwire*:
+.. Build a `FROM scratch` image carrying `.rbk/rbrd.env` and push it host-side to `«REGION»-docker.pkg.dev/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»/«GAR_REPOSITORY»/rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire`, authenticating to GAR with the Payor OAuth access token already in scope.
+.. {rbbc_require}
+pre-push existence guard via `docker manifest inspect`: image absent → proceed with build + push; image present → fatal (depot already inscribed; unmake and relevy to refresh).
+.. NOTE: The tripwire freezes the depot-time-immutable
+{rbrd_regime}
+(CLOUD_PREFIX, DEPOT_MONIKER, GCP_REGION, GCB_MACHINE_TYPE) into GAR. Every cloud-submitting command thereafter pulls and exact-byte-diffs the inscribed copy against local `.rbk/rbrd.env` before submitting, so a post-levy hand-edit fails loud rather than silently diverging from the depot's actual worker-pool quotas, region, and identity. Implemented host-side (Cloud Build is not involved) by `rbrd_inscribe` in `Tools/rbk/rbndb_base.sh`. The tripwire push runs after the depot-tracking refresh; a failure here leaves the depot live but un-inscribed, recoverable via `tt/rbw-rdi.sh` once the cause is resolved.
+
 //axhoc_completion
 Successful execution creates depot infrastructure with configured build bucket, container repository, Mason service account, and private worker pool — all derived from `(RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER)`.
 Proceed to
diff --git a/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRT-RegimeDepot.adoc b/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRT-RegimeDepot.adoc
index 1b8dcaab..b5e0ee55 100644
--- a/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRT-RegimeDepot.adoc
+++ b/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRT-RegimeDepot.adoc
@@ -83,3 +83,20 @@ Enforces format constraints on {rbrd_cloud_prefix} and {rbrd_depot_moniker}, and
 
 Renders the {rbrd_regime} grouped by section with presentation gates per group.
 Sections: Depot Identity, GCP Region, Google Cloud Build Pool.
+
+[[rbrd_tripwire]]
+{rbrd_tripwire}::
+A `FROM scratch` GAR image carrying the verbatim `.rbk/rbrd.env` assignment file, inscribed at `«REGION»-docker.pkg.dev/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»/«GAR_REPOSITORY»/rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire`.
+The tripwire makes the depot-time-immutable contract enforceable: because every RBDC-derived resource name (project ID, GAR repository, pool stem, bucket) is computed from the {rbrd_regime} at kindle time, a silent post-levy edit of `.rbk/rbrd.env` would point subsequent operations at a different depot identity or worker-pool configuration than the one actually provisioned.
++
+The tripwire is _produced_ exactly once, host-side, at the tail of
+{rbtgo_depot_levy}
+— after the depot-tracking refresh, using the Payor OAuth access token already in scope.
+A pre-push existence guard (`docker manifest inspect`) makes inscription idempotent-by-refusal: an already-present tripwire is fatal, since RBRD is frozen for the depot's productive lifetime and refreshing it requires a fresh depot (unmake + relevy).
++
+The tripwire is _consumed_ inline by every cloud-submitting command before it dispatches a build: the command pulls the image, extracts `/rbrd.env`, and exact-byte-compares it (comments, whitespace, and line ordering all participate) against the local `.rbk/rbrd.env`.
+A mismatch, a missing image, or a registry/auth failure is fatal with recovery guidance — the command refuses to submit cloud work against a drifted or unverifiable depot regime.
+The check takes a bearer token parameter and is role-agnostic; each consuming command passes its own role's token (no new IAM grants).
++
+The tripwire is depot-scoped operational state, not part of the hallmark / reliquary / enshrine image catalogue; the image enumerators ignore the `rbi_df` namespace by design.
+Both operations are implemented host-side (Cloud Build is not involved) by `rbrd_inscribe` and `rbrd_check` in `Tools/rbk/rbndb_base.sh`, exposed for manual use via the `rbw-rdi` (inscribe) and `rbw-rdc` (check) tabtargets.

addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Add RBRD tripwire documentation and validation

content.text:
Wire RBRD tripwire into the human handbook and three specs, completing pace BOAAK's documentation arc.

rbhopw_payor_wrapper.sh's payor handbook gains a tripwire section after the depot-list verification block: explains that successful levy inscribes the depot-immutable RBRD settings as a GAR image that every later cloud build byte-compares against local rbrd.env, then walks the three failure modes (drift, missing tripwire, re-inscribe refused) each with recovery commands, plus the manual rbw-rdc verification path.

RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc adds the "Inscribe Depot Regime Tripwire" step at the tail of levy: FROM-scratch image push host-side using the in-scope Payor token, manifest-inspect existence guard, and a NOTE explaining the freeze/consume contract and its rbrd_inscribe/rbndb_base.sh implementation.

RBSRT-RegimeDepot.adoc gains the rbrd_tripwire linked term (registered in RBS0's mapping section) — full definition covering production (once, host-side, at levy tail, idempotent-by-refusal), consumption (inline byte-compare at every cloud submit, role-agnostic token), the rbi_df-namespace exclusion from image catalogues, and the rbw-rdi/rbw-rdc tabtargets.

Note: I have not run anything to verify these doc changes; the message claims handbook-render coverage from the prior commit context but this diff is docs-only.

aiTitle: Add RBRD tripwire documentation and validation
